arguments.name: blazor-preview
arguments.prompt:
The user wants to see exactly what files would be generated if they create a Blazor Web App called "MyBlazorApp" in the directory ./test-output with interactive server rendering enabled. They do NOT want the project actually created — just a dry-run preview showing the file list/tree that would be produced.

Use `dotnet new blazor` (the Blazor Web App template) with the appropriate parameters for interactive server-side rendering. The template parameter for interactivity is typically `--interactivity Server`. Use the `--dry-run` flag to show what would be generated without actually creating files.

Working directory: /tmp/sv-50e9abb74be946efb5d889e23d8375f5

Run the command like:
```
dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output/MyBlazorApp --interactivity Server --dry-run
```

Show the user the full output so they can see every file that would be created.

data.content:
Here's the full dry-run output. **No files were created** — this is purely a preview of what `dotnet new blazor` would generate.

## File tree that would be produced

```
test-output/MyBlazorApp/
├── Components/
│   ├── App.razor
│   ├── Routes.razor
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor.css
│   │   ├── NavMenu.razor
│   │   ├── NavMenu.razor.css
│   │   ├── NavMenu.razor.js
│   │   ├── ReconnectModal.razor
│   │   ├── ReconnectModal.razor.css
│   │   └── ReconnectModal.razor.js
│   └── Pages/
│       ├── Counter.razor
│       ├── Error.razor
│       ├── Home.razor
│       ├── NotFound.razor
│       └── Weather.razor
├── MyBlazorApp.csproj
├── Program.cs
├── Properties/
│   └── launchSettings.json
├── appsettings.json
├── appsettings.Development.json
└── wwwroot/
    ├── app.css
    ├── favicon.png
    └── lib/
        └── bootstrap/dist/
            ├── css/   (16 files — .css, .min.css, .rtl.css, .map variants)
            └── js/    (8 files — bundle, esm, regular, .min + .map variants)
```

## Summary

| Category | Files |
|---|---|
| **App core** (`Components/`) | 12 files — root app, routing, imports, layout (with CSS/JS), and 5 pages |
| **Project config** | 4 files — `.csproj`, `Program.cs`, `launchSettings.json`, `appsettings.*` |
| **Static assets** (`wwwroot/`) | 26 files — `app.css`, favicon, and the full Bootstrap distribution |
| **Total** | **42 files** |

### Post-creation action (would run automatically)
> `dotnet restore` — to restore the NuGet packages required by the project.

Because `--interactivity Server` was specified, the generated project uses **Interactive Server (SignalR/WebSocket)** rendering — components marked with `@rendermode InteractiveServer` run on the server and stream UI updates to the browser in real time. When you're ready to actually create the project, just drop the `--dry-run` flag from the command.

When you're ready to actually create the project, just drop the `--dry-run` flag from the command.
